prompt:
Certificate renewals keep sneaking up on us â€” the wildcard for the edge expired on a Saturday again because the reminder lived in someone's head. I want a tiny per-directory renewal ledger driven by one PowerShell script, `renew.ps1`, in the repo root. Three commands, git-style: `renew.ps1 init`, `renew.ps1 add ...`, `renew.ps1 report ...`.

Structure opinion, non-negotiable: ONE `switch` on the command word dispatching to a small function per command, and the per-command options parsed into a hashtable that gets SPLATTED into that function (`Invoke-Add @opts`). I've maintained the six-copies-of-arg-parsing kind of script before and I'm not doing it again.

Storage: `renewals.json` in the current directory â€” a JSON object with an `entries` array; each entry has `name`, `expiresOn`, `owner`. That format is a contract (the report tooling on the dashboard side reads it), so keep it exactly that shape.

Commands:
- `init` â€” create the store here. stdout `initialized renewals.json`, exit 0. Already exists: stderr `renew: already initialized`, exit 65.
- `add -Name <cert> -ExpiresOn <yyyy-MM-dd> [-Owner <team>]` â€” append an entry; owner defaults to `-`. stdout `added <name> (expires <date>)`. Names must match `^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]*$` or it's `renew: invalid certificate name: <v>`, exit 65. Dates must be shaped `yyyy-MM-dd` or `renew: invalid date: <v>`, 65. Duplicate name (case-SENSITIVE compare â€” `edge-tls` and `Edge-tls` are different certs, annoyingly real): `renew: duplicate certificate: <name>`, 65. Missing or empty -Name/-ExpiresOn: `renew: add: -Name and -ExpiresOn are required` + usage, 64. Run before init: `renew: not initialized (run 'renew.ps1 init' first)`, 65.
- `report [-Before <yyyy-MM-dd>] [-Owner <team>]` â€” print matching entries, one per line, `<expiresOn> <name> <owner>` separated by single spaces, sorted by expiry ascending and then by name â€” ORDINAL both, not the default culture sort (I got burned by `Sort-Object` putting `api-signer` ahead of `Zeta-cache` once; never again). `-Before` keeps strictly-earlier expiries (the boundary date itself is out). `-Owner` is an exact match. Bad -Before value: `renew: invalid date: <v>`, 65. No matches: print nothing, exit 0. Before init: same 65 as add.
- `help` â€” usage block to stdout, exit 0.

Shared option-parsing rules for add/report: unknown `-Something` is `renew: unknown option: -Something` + usage, 64. An option at the end with no value following: `renew: option -Something requires a value` + usage, 64. A bare word where an option should be: `renew: <command>: unexpected argument: <word>` + usage, 64. No command at all: `renew: missing command` + usage, 64. Unknown command word: `renew: unknown command: <word>` + usage, 64.

Exit contract: 0 ok, 64 usage error (one-line message then the usage block, both stderr), 65 data error (message only, stderr).

The usage block, byte-exact (tests compare it verbatim):

usage: renew.ps1 <command> [options]

commands:
  init      create renewals.json in the current directory
  add       -Name <cert> -ExpiresOn <yyyy-MM-dd> [-Owner <team>]
  report    [-Before <yyyy-MM-dd>] [-Owner <team>]
  help      print this help

exit codes: 0 ok, 64 usage error, 65 data error

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_renew.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified â€” don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P1 item 2: subcommand dispatcher via switch on $args[0] + per-command splatting from a hashtable. Differentiation from bash-subcmd (per-directory maintenance log, getopts + tab-separated flat file): different layer entirely â€” JSON store parsed/serialized structurally, hashtable splatting instead of getopts, case-sensitivity pins (-ceq duplicate check, ordinal report sort with a mixed-case fixture 'Zeta-cache.internal' vs 'api-signer' that a culture Sort-Object would swap â€” probed 2026-07-14: default Sort-Object is culture-linguistic). Domain (TLS renewal ledger) collides with no existing seed (csharp-toollend is equipment lending, py-library-lending is books).
